title: File Servers, Object Storage, CDNs, and Architecture Selection · Systems Transfer
summary: An independent team receives different tenants, file sizes, media types, regions, compliance duties, traffic, failures, and cost limits and transfers file servers, object storage, cdns, and architecture selection into a new architecture and responsibility decision record. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of architecture and responsibility decision record with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case prac…
objectives: Compare local filesystems, shared filesystems, object storage, databases, origin services, and CDNs by interface, consistency, scale, trust, and operations.
Separate control-plane metadata and authorization from high-volume data transfer while preserving one auditable decision.
Choose server upload, direct upload, redirect, proxy, signed edge delivery, or public delivery from threat, transformation, audit, and performance evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Commit to the mission model · Lab Filesystem Object Cdn
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab Go file-delivery team handles case 9833 during a private media release. Build deterministic Go evidence to select a file, object, gateway, direct, or CDN path from trust and volume evidence; retain stable tenant and file identity without exposing it in metric labels; then change region, range, or signed header and explain the exact failure boundary. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Compare local filesystems, shared filesystems, object storage, databases, origin services, and CDNs by interface, consistency, scale, trust, and operations.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: S3, CloudFront, and Go Commit to the mission model · Lab Filesystem Object Cdn.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for compare local filesystems, shared filesystems, object storage, databases, origin services, and cdns by interface, consistency, scale, trust, and operations. For S3, CloudFront, and Go Commit to the mission model · Lab Filesystem Object Cdn, keep Filesystem Object Cdn connected to executable Go storage and delivery decision evidence.

Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Compare local filesystems, shared filesystems, object storage, databases, origin services, and CDNs by interface, consistency, scale, trust, and operations.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Control Data Plane · changed case · Lab Control Data Plane
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab Go file-delivery team handles case 2727 during an SDK and infrastructure canary. Build deterministic Go evidence to select a file, object, gateway, direct, or CDN path from trust and volume evidence; do not treat an ETag, CORS result, emulator, or successful SDK call as stronger proof than it is; then cancel halfway through and prove every body, part, and goroutine reaches a terminal state. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Separate control-plane metadata and authorization from high-volume data transfer while preserving one auditable decision.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Cost Failure Model
steps.instruction:
Append a compile-ready pure-Go function headed "// Evidence: storage-go-cost-failure-model-157k5y" that uses case 6987 to select a file, object, gateway, direct, or CDN path from trust and volume evidence. Return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not import AWS SDK packages, open sockets, contact S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, networks, databases, or external effects, read host state, execute host commands, or use credentials; verify those boundaries later with Go 1.26.5 compile, vet, fuzz, race, leak, controlled AWS, load, fault, security, cost, restore, and production gates.

Evidence target: S3, CloudFront, and Go Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Cost Failure Model.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Cost Failure Model into observable performance evidence. For S3, CloudFront, and Go Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Cost Failure Model, keep Cost Failure Model connected to executable Go storage and delivery decision evidence.

content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The structural check is only one gate. The following changed-case decision and explanation must also hold.

hints: For S3, CloudFront, and Go Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Cost Failure Model, name the stakeholder, file, object version, authorization, byte, time, concurrency, cache, cost, cleanup, and recovery boundary, Cost Failure Model precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At S3, CloudFront, and Go Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Cost Failure Model, trace this concrete input and predict authorization, custody, integrity, range, cache, retry, cost, cleanup, and recovery evidence before reviewing it: A lab Go file-delivery team handles case 8928 during a regional dependency incident. Build deterministic Go evidence to select a file, object, gateway, direct, or CDN path from trust and volume evidence; do not treat an ETag, CORS result, emulator, or successful SDK call as stronger proof than it is; then change cache and retrieval cost assumptions and defend the new budget result. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Model storage, request, transfer, retrieval, invalidation, compute, and operational cost together with regional and dependency failures.

For S3, CloudFront, and Go Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Cost Failure Model, challenge the documented misconception with file, object, policy, transfer, integrity, cache, fault, cost, restore, and controlled AWS transfer evidence: S3 storage price alone determines the cheapest architecture.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Test an acceptance boundary · Lab Cost Failure Model
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab Go file-delivery team handles case 4785 during an SDK and infrastructure canary. Build deterministic Go evidence to select a file, object, gateway, direct, or CDN path from trust and volume evidence; preserve accessible progress, cancellation, status, and recovery evidence; then cancel halfway through and prove every body, part, and goroutine reaches a terminal state. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Model storage, request, transfer, retrieval, invalidation, compute, and operational cost together with regional and dependency failures.

One precondition fails while output still looks plausible. What is the correct response?

Evidence target: S3, CloudFront, and Go Test an acceptance boundary · Lab Cost Failure Model.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for model storage, request, transfer, retrieval, invalidation, compute, and operational cost together with regional and dependency failures. For S3, CloudFront, and Go Test an acceptance boundary · Lab Cost Failure Model, keep Cost Failure Model connected to executable Go storage and delivery decision evidence.

stimulus.title: Cost Failure Model failure evidence
stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

lines.label: observed
lines.text: S3 storage price alone determines the cheapest architecture.

lines.label: required
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Model storage, request, transfer, retrieval, invalidation, compute, and operational cost together with regional and dependency failures.

options.text: Keep the plausible output and hide the assumption to avoid confusing the stakeholder. That claim does not survive the test an acceptance boundary evidence boundary.
For test an acceptance boundary, run one convenient Cost Failure Model happy path, omit ownership, error, changed input, goroutine stop, and full-toolchain checks, then accept plausible output.
Reject the unsupported conclusion, name the violated Cost Failure Model condition, repair the responsible layer, and rerun the boundary trace.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Defend the transferred model · Lab Cost Failure Model
steps.instruction:

Build deterministic Go evidence to select a file, object, gateway, direct, or CDN path from trust and volume evidence; preserve accessible progress, cancellation, status, and recovery evidence; then change region, range, or signed header and explain the exact failure boundary. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Model storage, request, transfer, retrieval, invalidation, compute, and operational cost together with regional and dependency failures.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Model storage, request, transfer, retrieval, invalidation, compute, and operational cost together with regional and dependency failures.
